pattern: Imperative Shell\n\nmod run_output;\n\nuse std::env;\nuse std::fs::File;\nuse std::io::{self, BufWriter, Write};\nuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\n\nuse anyhow::Context;\nuse clap::{Parser, Subcommand};\nuse futures::StreamExt;\nuse halter::prelude::*;\nuse halter_config::{export_json_schema, generate_starter_config, load_path};\nuse halter_protocol::{AssistantMessage, SessionEvent, SessionEventPayload};\nuse run_output::{\n    JsonResultTracker, RunOutputArgs, RunOutputMode, strip_signatures_from_assistant_message,\n    strip_signatures_from_session_event,\n};\nuse tokio::io::{AsyncBufReadExt, BufReader};\nuse tracing::{debug, info};\nuse tracing_subscriber::{EnvFilter, fmt, prelude::*};\n\n#[derive(Debug, Parser)]\n#[command(name = \"halter\")]\n#[command(about = \"Lightweight Rust agent harness SDK and portable binary\")]\nstruct Cli {\n    #[arg(long, default_value = \"halter.toml\")]\n    config: PathBuf,\n    #[arg(\n        long,\n        global = true,\n        help = \"Write CLI output to a file instead of standard output\"\n    )]\n    output_file: Option<PathBuf>,\n    #[command(subcommand)]\n    command: Commands,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Subcommand)]\nenum Commands {\n    Init,\n    Chat,\n    Run {\n        #[command(flatten)]\n        output: RunOutputArgs,\n        task: String,\n    },\n    Resources,\n    Validate,\n    Config {\n        #[command(subcommand)]\n        command: ConfigCommands,\n    },\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Subcommand)]\nenum ConfigCommands {\n    Schema,\n}\n\n#[tokio::main]\nasync fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n    init_logging()?;\n    let cli = Cli::parse();\n    debug!(config_path = %cli.config.display(), command = ?cli.command, \"parsed cli arguments\");\n    let mut output = open_output_writer(cli.output_file.as_deref())?;\n\n    match cli.command {\n        Commands::Init => init_config(&cli.config, output.as_mut()).await,\n        Commands::Chat => chat(&cli.config, output.as_mut()).await,\n        Commands::Run {\n            task,\n            output: run_output,\n        } => run_once(&cli.config, &task, run_output.mode(), output.as_mut()).await,\n        Commands::Resources => show_resources(&cli.config, output.as_mut()).await,\n        Commands::Validate => validate(&cli.config, output.as_mut()).await,\n        Commands::Config {\n            command: ConfigCommands::Schema,\n        } => {\n            write_output_line(output.as_mut(), export_json_schema()?)?;\n            Ok(())\n        }\n    }?;\n\n    output.flush().context(\"failed to flush output\")\n}\n\nasync fn init_config(path: &Path, output: &mut dyn Write) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n    info!(path = %path.display(), \"initializing starter config\");\n    if path.exists() {\n        anyhow::bail!(\n            \"failed to initialize config: {} already exists\",\n            path.display()\n        );\n    }\n    tokio::fs::write(path, generate_starter_config())\n        .await\n        .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to write {}\", path.display()))?;\n    write_output_line(output, format!(\"wrote {}\", path.display()))\n}\n\nasync fn validate(path: &Path, output: &mut dyn Write) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n    info!(path = %path.display(), \"validating config\");\n    load_path(path).await?;\n    write_output_line(output, \"config valid\")\n}\n\nasync fn show_resources(path: &Path, output: &mut dyn Write) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n    info!(path = %path.display(), \"compiling resources\");\n    let config = load_path(path).await?;\n    let resources = ResourceCompiler::from_config(&config).compile().await?;\n    write_output_line(\n        output,\n        format!(\"revision: {}\", resources.snapshot.revision.0),\n    )?;\n    write_output_line(\n        output,\n        format!(\"skills: {}\", resources.snapshot.skills.len()),\n    )?;\n    write_output_line(\n        output,\n        format!(\"agents: {}\", resources.snapshot.agents.len()),\n    )?;\n    write_output_line(\n        output,\n        format!(\"plugins: {}\", resources.snapshot.plugins.len()),\n    )\n}\n\nasync fn run_once(\n    path: &Path,\n    task: &str,\n    output_mode: RunOutputMode,\n    output: &mut dyn Write,\n) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n    info!(\n        path = %path.display(),\n        output_mode = ?output_mode,\n        task_len = task.len(),\n        \"running single turn\"\n    );\n    let harness = Halter::from_config_file(path).await?;\n    let session = harness.new_session(SessionInit::default()).await?;\n    let mut events = session.submit_turn(Turn::user(task)).await?;\n\n    match output_mode {\n        RunOutputMode::StreamingJson => {\n            while let Some(event) = events.next().await {\n                write_json_event(output, &event?)?;\n            }\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        RunOutputMode::JsonResult => {\n            let mut tracker = JsonResultTracker::default();\n            while let Some(event) = events.next().await {\n                let event = event?;\n                if let Some(result) = tracker\n                    .observe(&event.payload)\n                    .map_err(anyhow::Error::msg)?\n                {\n                    write_json_result(output, result)?;\n                    return Ok(());\n                }\n            }\n            anyhow::bail!(\"failed to receive final assistant result\")\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nasync fn chat(path: &Path, output: &mut dyn Write) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n    info!(path = %path.display(), \"starting chat session\");\n    let harness = Halter::from_config_file(path).await?;\n    let session = harness.new_session(SessionInit::default()).await?;\n    let stdin = BufReader::new(tokio::io::stdin());\n    let mut lines = stdin.lines();\n\n    write_output_line(\n        output,\n        \"halter chat; submit an empty line or press ctrl-d to exit\",\n    )?;\n    while let Some(line) = lines.next_line().await.context(\"failed to read stdin\")? {\n        if line.trim().is_empty() {\n            break;\n        }\n\n        let mut events = session.submit_turn(Turn::user(line)).await?;\n        while let Some(event) = events.next().await {\n            match event?.payload {\n                SessionEventPayload::DeltaItem { delta } => {\n                    write!(output, \"{}\", delta.text).context(\"failed to write output\")?;\n                    output.flush().context(\"failed to flush output\")?;\n                }\n                SessionEventPayload::ToolOutput { chunk, .. } => {\n                    write!(output, \"{}\", chunk).context(\"failed to write output\")?;\n                    output.flush().context(\"failed to flush output\")?;\n                }\n                SessionEventPayload::TurnCompleted { .. } => {\n                    writeln!(output).context(\"failed to write output\")?;\n                    output.flush().context(\"failed to flush output\")?;\n                    break;\n                }\n                SessionEventPayload::TurnFailed { error, .. } => anyhow::bail!(error),\n                _ => {}\n            }\n        }\n    }\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn write_json_event(output: &mut dyn Write, event: &SessionEvent) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n    let event = strip_signatures_from_session_event(event);\n    serde_json::to_writer(&mut *output, &event).context(\"failed to serialize session event\")?;\n    writeln!(output).context(\"failed to write output\")?;\n    output.flush().context(\"failed to flush output\")\n}\n\nfn write_json_result(output: &mut dyn Write, result: &AssistantMessage) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n    let result = anyhow::Result<()> {\n    init_logging()?;\n    let cli = Cli::parse();\n    debug!(config_path = %cli.config.display(), command = ?cli.command, \"parsed cli arguments\");\n    let mut output = Commands::Resources => show_resources(&cli.config, output.as_mut()).await,\n        Commands::Validate => validate(&cli.config, output.as_mut()).await,\n        Commands::Config {\n            command: ConfigCommands::Schema,\n        } => {\n            write_output_line(output.as_mut(), export_json_schema()?)?;\n            Ok(())\n        }\n    }?;\n\n    output.flush().context(\"failed to flush output\")\n}\n\nasync fn init_config(path: &Path, output: &mut dyn Write) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n    info!(path = %path.display(), \"initializing starter config\");\n    if path.exists() {\n        anyhow::bail!(\n            \"failed to initialize config: {} already exists\",\n            path.display()\n        );\n    }\n    tokio::fs::write(path, generate_starter_config())\n        .await\n        .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to write {}\", path.display()))?;\n    write_output_line(output, format!(\"wrote {}\", path.display()))\n}\n\nasync fn validate(path: &Path, output: &mut dyn Write) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n    info!(path = %path.display(), \"validating config\");\n    load_path(path).await?;\n    write_output_line(output, \"config valid\")\n}\n\nasync fn ResourceCompiler::from_config(&config).compile().await?;\n    write_output_line(\n        output,\n        format!(\"revision: {}\", resources.snapshot.revision.0),\n    )?;\n    write_output_line(\n        output,\n        format!(\"skills: {}\", resources.snapshot.skills.len()),\n    )?;\n    write_output_line(\n        output,\n        format!(\"agents: {}\", resources.snapshot.agents.len()),\n RunOutputMode,\n    output: &mut dyn Write,\n) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n    info!(\n        path = %path.display(),\n        output_mode = ?output_mode,\n        task_len = task.len(),\n        \"running single turn\"\n    );\n    let harness = Halter::from_config_file(path).await?;\n    let session = harness.new_session(SessionInit::default()).await?;\n    let mut events = session.submit_turn(Turn::user(task)).await?;\n\n    match output_mode {\n        RunOutputMode::StreamingJson => {\n            while let Some(event) = events.next().await {\n \"starting chat session\");\n    let harness = Halter::from_config_file(path).await?;\n    let session = harness.new_session(SessionInit::default()).await?;\n    let stdin = BufReader::new(tokio::io::stdin());\n    let mut lines = stdin.lines();\n\n    write_output_line(\n        output,\n        \"halter chat; submit an empty line or press ctrl-d to exit\",\n    )?;\n session.submit_turn(Turn::user(line)).await?;\n        while let Some(event) = events.next().await {\n            match event?.payload {\n                SessionEventPayload::DeltaItem { delta } => flush output\")?;\n                }\n                SessionEventPayload::TurnCompleted { .. } => {\n                    writeln!(output).context(\"failed to write output\")?;\n                    output.flush().context(\"failed to flush output\")?;\n                    break;\n                }\n                SessionEventPayload::TurnFailed { error, .. } => anyhow::bail!(error),\n                _ => {}\n            }\n        }\n    }\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn write_json_event(output: &mut dyn Write, event: &SessionEvent) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n    let event = strip_signatures_from_session_event(event);\n    serde_json::to_writer(&mut *output, &event).context(\"failed to serialize session event\")?;\n    writeln!(output).context(\"failed to write output\")?;\n    output.flush().context(\"failed to flush output\")\n}\n\nfn write_json_result(output: &mut dyn Write, result: &AssistantMessage) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n    let result = {\n        #[command(subcommand)]\n        command: ConfigCommands,\n    },\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Subcommand)]\nenum ConfigCommands {\n    Schema,\n}\n\n#[tokio::main]\nasync fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n    init_logging()?;\n    let cli = Cli::parse();\n    debug!(config_path = %cli.config.display(), command = ?cli.command, \"parsed cli arguments\");\n    let mut output = output.as_mut()).await,\n        Commands::Run {\n            task,\n            output: run_output,\n        } => run_once(&cli.config, &task, run_output.mode(), output.as_mut()).await,\n        Commands::Resources => show_resources(&cli.config, output.as_mut()).await,\n        Commands::Validate => validate(&cli.config, output.as_mut()).await,\n        Commands::Config {\n            command: ConfigCommands::Schema,\n        } => {\n            write_output_line(output.as_mut(), export_json_schema()?)?;\n            Ok(())\n        }\n    }?;\n\n    output.flush().context(\"failed &mut dyn Write) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n    info!(path = %path.display(), \"validating config\");\n    load_path(path).await?;\n    write_output_line(output, \"config valid\")\n}\n\nasync fn show_resources(path: &Path, output: &mut dyn Write) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n    info!(path = %path.display(), \"compiling resources\");\n    let config = load_path(path).await?;\n    let resources = ResourceCompiler::from_config(&config).compile().await?;\n    write_output_line(\n        output,\n        format!(\"revision: {}\", resources.snapshot.revision.0),\n    )?;\n    write_output_line(\n        output,\n        format!(\"skills: {}\", resources.snapshot.skills.len()),\n    )?;\n    write_output_line(\n        output,\n        format!(\"agents: {}\", resources.snapshot.agents.len()),\n    )?;\n    write_output_line(\n        output,\n        format!(\"plugins: {}\", resources.snapshot.plugins.len()),\n    )\n}\n\nasync fn run_once(\n    path: &Path,\n    task: &str,\n    output_mode: RunOutputMode,\n    output: &mut dyn Write,\n) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n    info!(\n        path = %path.display(),\n        output_mode = ?output_mode,\n        task_len = task.len(),\n        \"running single turn\"\n    );\n    let harness = Halter::from_config_file(path).await?;\n    let session = harness.new_session(SessionInit::default()).await?;\n    let mut events = session.submit_turn(Turn::user(task)).await?;\n\n    match output_mode {\n        RunOutputMode::StreamingJson => {\n            while let Some(event) = events.next().await {\n                write_json_event(output, &event?)?;\n            }\n            Ok(())\n        }\n        RunOutputMode::JsonResult => {\n            let mut tracker = JsonResultTracker::default();\n            while let Some(event) = events.next().await {\n                let event = event?;\n                if let Some(result) = tracker\n                    .observe(&event.payload)\n                    .map_err(anyhow::Error::msg)?\n                {\n                    write_json_result(output, result)?;\n                    return Ok(());\n                }\n            }\n \"starting chat session\");\n    let harness = Halter::from_config_file(path).await?;\n    let session = harness.new_session(SessionInit::default()).await?;\n    let stdin = BufReader::new(tokio::io::stdin());\n    let mut lines = stdin.lines();\n\n    write_output_line(\n        output,\n        \"halter chat; submit an empty line or press ctrl-d to exit\",\n    )?;\n flush output\")?;\n                }\n                SessionEventPayload::TurnCompleted { .. } => {\n                    writeln!(output).context(\"failed to write output\")?;\n                    output.flush().context(\"failed to flush output\")?;\n                    break;\n                }\n                SessionEventPayload::TurnFailed { error, .. } => anyhow::bail!(error),\n                _ => {}\n            }\n        }\n    }\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nfn write_json_event(output: &mut dyn Write, event: &SessionEvent) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n    let event = strip_signatures_from_session_event(event);\n    serde_json::to_writer(&mut *output, &event).context(\"failed to serialize session event\")?;\n    writeln!(output).context(\"failed to write output\")?;\n    output.flush().context(\"failed to flush output\")\n}\n\nfn write_json_result(output: &mut dyn Write, result: &AssistantMessage) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n    let result = strip_signatures_from_assistant_message(result);\n    serde_json::to_writer(&mut *output, &result).context(\"failed to serialize assistant result\")?;\n    writeln!(output).context(\"failed to write output\")?;\n    output.flush().context(\"failed to flush output\")\n}\n\nfn open_output_writer(path: Option<&Path>) -> anyhow::Result<Box<dyn Write>> {\n    match path {\n        Some(path) => {\n            let file = File::create(path)\n                .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to create output file {}\", path.display()))?;\n            Ok(Box::new(BufWriter::new(file)))\n        }\n        None => Ok(Box::new(io::stdout())),\n    }\n}\n\nfn write_output_line(output: &mut dyn Write, line: impl std::fmt::Display) -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n    writeln!(output, \"{line}\").context(\"failed to write output\")\n}\n\nfn init_logging() -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n    let filter = match env::var(EnvFilter::DEFAULT_ENV) {\n        Ok(value) => EnvFilter::try_new(value).context(\"invalid RUST_LOG filter\")?,\n        Err(env::VarError::NotPresent) => EnvFilter::try_new(\"off\")?,\n        Err(env::VarError::NotUnicode(_)) => anyhow::bail!(\"invalid utf-8 in RUST_LOG\"),\n    };\n    let subscriber = tracing_subscriber::registry().with(filter).with(\n        fmt::layer()\n            .with_writer(std::io::stderr)\n            .with_target(true)\n            .compact(),\n    );\n    tracing::subscriber::set_global_default(subscriber).context(\"failed to initialize logging\")\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use  \"content\": system,\n        }));\n    }\n\n    for message in &request.messages {\n        match message {\n            Message::System(_) => {}\n            Message::User(user) => Value::String(text));\n                } else if tool_calls.is_empty() {\n                    continue;\n                }\n                if !tool_calls.is_empty() {\n assistant_message.insert(\"tool_calls\".to_owned(), Value::Array(tool_calls));\n                }\n                messages.push(Value::Object(assistant_message));\n            }\n            Message::Tool(tool) => messages.push(json!({\n                \"role\": \"tool\",\n                \"tool_call_id\": tool.call_id,\n                \"content\": tool_result_text(&tool.content, &tool.error),\n            })),\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(messages)\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nfn encode_chat_user_message(message: &halter_protocol::UserMessage) -> anyhow::Result<Value> {\n    if !has_user_media(message) {\n        return Ok(json!({\n            \"role\": \"user\",\n            \"content\": user_text(message),\n        }));\n    }\n\n    let mut content = Vec::new();\n    for part in &message.parts {\n        match part {\n            UserPart::Text { text } => content.push(json!({\n                \"type\": \"text\",\n                \"text\": text,\n            })),\n            UserPart::Image { media_type, data } => content.push(json!({\n                \"type\": \"image_url\",\n                \"image_url\": {\n                    \"url\": data_url(media_type, data),\n                }\n            })),\n            UserPart::Document { media_type, .. } => {\n                anyhow::bail!(\n                    \"failed to encode openai chat request: document inputs are not supported by chat completions ({media_type})\"\n                );\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(json!({\n        \"role\": \"user\",\n        \"content\": content,\n    }))\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nfn encode_chat_tools(request: &ProviderRequest) -> Vec<Value> {\n    request\n        .tools\n        .iter()\n        .map(|tool| {\n            json!({\n reasoning: Option<ReasoningEffort>,\n    summary: Option<&str>,\n) -> Option<Value> {\n    let effort = match reasoning? {\n        ReasoningEffort::Low => \"low\",\n        ReasoningEffort::Medium => &Value, saw_tool_call: bool) -> StopReason {\n    if saw_tool_call {\n        return StopReason::ToolUse;\n    }\n    match response\n        .pointer(\"/incomplete_details/reason\")\n        .and_then(Value::as_str)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n    {\n        \"max_output_tokens\" | \"max_completion_tokens\" => StopReason::MaxTokens,\n        _ => StopReason::EndTurn,\n    }\n}\n\nfn response\n        .incomplete_details\n        .as_ref()\n        .map(|details| details.reason.as_str())\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n    {\n        \"max_output_tokens\" | \"max_completion_tokens\" => StopReason::MaxTokens,\n        _ => StopReason::EndTurn,\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nfn decode_chat_stop_reason(choice: &Value) -> StopReason {\n    match choice\n        .get(\"finish_reason\")\n        .and_then(Value::as_str)\n        .unwrap_or(\"stop\")\n    {\n        \"tool_calls\" | \"function_call\" => StopReason::ToolUse,\n        \"length\" => StopReason::MaxTokens,\n        \"content_filter\" => StopReason::Error,\n        _ => StopReason::EndTurn,\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nfn decode_openai_usage(response: &Value) -> Usage {\n    Usage {\n        input_tokens: response\n            .pointer(\"/usage/input_tokens\")\n            .and_then(Value::as_u64)\n            .unwrap_or_default(),\n        output_tokens: response\n            .pointer(\"/usage/output_tokens\")\n            .and_then(Value::as_u64)\n            .unwrap_or_default(),\n        cache_creation_input_tokens: response\n .pointer(\"/usage/input_tokens_details/cached_tokens\")\n            .and_then(Value::as_u64)\n            .unwrap_or_default(),\n    }\n}\n\nfn decode_responses_usage(usage:      self\n    }\n\n    #[must_use]\n    pub fn with_plugin_hook_priority(\n        mut self,\n        plugin_id: halter_protocol::PluginId,\n        priority: RegisteredHookPriority,\n        hook: self\n    }\n\n    pub async fn build(self) -> anyhow::Result<Halter> {\n        let config = self.config;\n        debug!(\"validating halter builder config\");\n        config.validate()?;\n        self.registered_hooks.validate()?;\n\n        if !self.loaded_skills.is_empty() || !self.loaded_plugins.is_empty() {\n            anyhow::bail!(\n                \"failed to build halter runtime: loaded skills/plugins require a prebuilt resource snapshot\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        let snapshot = self.resource_snapshot.with_context(|| {\n            \"failed to build halter runtime: missing resource snapshot; use Halter::from_config_file or HalterBuilder::with_resource_snapshot\"\n        })?;\n        let hooks = self\n            .resource_hooks\n            .unwrap_or_else(|| Arc::new(Hooks::default()));\n        let hook_warnings = self.resource_hook_warnings;\n\n        let models = Arc::new(build_model_registry(&config)?);\n        let tools = Arc::new(ToolRuntime::new());\n        register_builtin_tools(&tools, &config.tools.enabled);\n        for tool in self.tools {\n            tools.register(tool);\n        }\n\n        let policy = Arc::new(DefaultToolPolicy::new(policy_from_config(&config.policy)));\n        let session_backend = self\n            .session_store\n            .as_ref()\n            .map(|_| \"custom\".to_owned())\n            .unwrap_or_else(|| describe_session_backend(&config.sessions).to_owned());\n        let sessions = match self.session_store {\n            Some(store) => store,\n            None => build_session_store(&config.sessions)?,\n        };\n        let services = Arc::new(RuntimeServices {\n            resources: Arc::new(ResourceHandle::new(snapshot, hooks, hook_warnings)),\n            registered_hooks: Arc::new(self.registered_hooks),\n            session_hook_store: Arc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(std::collections::HashMap::new())),\n            models,\n            tools,\n            path_locks: Arc::new(PathLockMap::default()),\n            tool_sessions: Arc::new(ToolSessionStore::default()),\n            sessions,\n            policy: policy.clone(),\n            prompt_assembler: Arc::new(DefaultPromptAssembler),\n            context_manager: Arc::new(DefaultContextManager::new(\n                config.context.max_context_messages,\n            )),\n            event_bus: Arc::new(EventBus::default()),\n            max_tool_output_bytes: config.policy.max_tool_output_bytes,\n            shell_timeout_secs: config.policy.shell.timeout_secs,\n        });\n        let runtime = SessionRuntime::new(services.clone());\n        register_subagent_tools(\n            &services.tools,\n            runtime.subagent_control(),\n            &config.tools.enabled,\n        );\n        let default_model = config.default_model()?;\n        let subagent_model = config.subagent_model().unwrap_or(default_model);\n        info!(\n            default_provider = %default_model.provider,\n            default_model = %default_model.model,\n            subagent_provider = %subagent_model.provider,\n            subagent_model = %subagent_model.model,\n            session_backend,\n            tool_count = services.tools.specs().len(),\n            snapshot_revision = %services.resources.snapshot().revision,\n            \"built halter runtime\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(Halter { config, runtime })\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct   self.tools.push(tool);\n        self\n    }\n\n    #[must_use]\n    pub fn with_session_store(mut self, store: Arc<dyn SessionStore>) -> Self {\n        self.session_store = Some(store);\n        self\n    }\n\n    pub async fn build(self) -> anyhow::Result<Halter> {\n        let config = self.config;\n        debug!(\"validating halter builder config\");\n        config.validate()?;\n        self.registered_hooks.validate()?;\n\n        if !self.loaded_skills.is_empty() || !self.loaded_plugins.is_empty() {\n            anyhow::bail!(\n                \"failed to build halter runtime: loaded skills/plugins require a prebuilt resource snapshot\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        let snapshot = self.resource_snapshot.with_context(|| {\n            \"failed to build halter runtime: missing resource snapshot; use Halter::from_config_file or HalterBuilder::with_resource_snapshot\"\n        })?;\n        let hooks = self\n            .resource_hooks\n            .unwrap_or_else(|| Arc::new(Hooks::default()));\n        let hook_warnings = self.resource_hook_warnings;\n\n        let models = Arc::new(build_model_registry(&config)?);\n        let tools = Arc::new(ToolRuntime::new());\n        register_builtin_tools(&tools, &config.tools.enabled);\n        for tool in self.tools {\n            tools.register(tool);\n        }\n\n        let policy = Arc::new(DefaultToolPolicy::new(policy_from_config(&config.policy)));\n        let session_backend = self\n            .session_store\n            .as_ref()\n            .map(|_| \"custom\".to_owned())\n            .unwrap_or_else(|| describe_session_backend(&config.sessions).to_owned());\n        let sessions = match self.session_store {\n            Some(store) => store,\n            None => build_session_store(&config.sessions)?,\n        };\n        let services = Arc::new(RuntimeServices {\n            resources: Arc::new(ResourceHandle::new(snapshot, hooks, hook_warnings)),\n            registered_hooks: Arc::new(self.registered_hooks),\n            session_hook_store: Arc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(std::collections::HashMap::new())),\n            models,\n            tools,\n            path_locks: Arc::new(PathLockMap::default()),\n            tool_sessions: Arc::new(ToolSessionStore::default()),\n            sessions,\n            policy: policy.clone(),\n            prompt_assembler: Arc::new(DefaultPromptAssembler),\n            context_manager: Arc::new(DefaultContextManager::new(\n                config.context.max_context_messages,\n            )),\n            event_bus: Arc::new(EventBus::default()),\n            max_tool_output_bytes: config.policy.max_tool_output_bytes,\n            shell_timeout_secs: config.policy.shell.timeout_secs,\n        });\n        let runtime = SessionRuntime::new(services.clone());\n        register_subagent_tools(\n            &services.tools,\n            runtime.subagent_control(),\n            &config.tools.enabled,\n        );\n        let default_model = config.default_model()?;\n        let subagent_model = config.subagent_model().unwrap_or(default_model);\n        info!(\n            default_provider = %default_model.provider,\n            default_model = %default_model.model,\n            subagent_provider = %subagent_model.provider,\n            subagent_model = %subagent_model.model,\n            session_backend,\n            tool_count = services.tools.specs().len(),\n            snapshot_revision = %services.resources.snapshot().revision,\n            \"built halter runtime\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(Halter { config, runtime })\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct Vec::new();\n    if let Some(system) = collect_system_text(request) {\n        messages.push(json!({\n            \"role\": \"system\",\n            \"content\": system,\n        }));\n    }\n\n            .filter_map(|part| match part {\n                        AssistantPart::ToolCall(call) => Some(json!({\n                            \"id\": call.id,\n                            \"type\":            &request.tools,\n                                    request.model.provider_kind,\n                                ),\n                                \"arguments\": call.arguments.to_string(),\n                            }\n                        })),\n                        AssistantPart::Text { .. } | AssistantPart::Thinking(_) => None,\n                    })\n                    .collect::<Vec<_>>();\n                let mut assistant_message = Map::new();\n                assistant_message.insert(\"role\".to_owned(), json!(\"assistant\"));\n                if !text.is_empty() {\n                    assistant_message.insert(\"content\".to_owned(), Value::String(text));\n                } else if tool_calls.is_empty() {\n
